raw_consumer_statement: b'On XXXX/25 I went to Macy\'s and picked out 2 sets of sheets for XXXX  each and a pajama bottom for XXXXXXXX.  I shopped at Macy\'s because I had a Macy\'s Start Rewards card for a XXXX discount.  I believed that they needed my card to validate the rewards card so I gave the clerk both the discount card and my Macy\'s credit card.  I was told the the discount card was invalid and I could not receive the discount.  I informed the clerk that I didn\'t want to proceed with the sale and I turned to walk away.  I realized that I really needed the pajamas so I gave the clerk my Mastercard and asked to "ring that up".  I left the store with the pajamas and no sheets.  They were left on the counter.  When I received my Macy\'s bill I was incorrectly charged XXXX for all 3 items.  I filed an immediate dispute and gave the complete story to an agent who told me that He would enter the information in the dispute and He further said that the dispute should be honored.  I received notice that XXXX  for the pajamas would be refunded.  I sent an email to XXXX  that I have attached.  I then received notice that my dispute was resolved and I have received a credit of XXXXXXXX  which would show up within 2 billing periods.  That is also attached.  The next bill came with no credit.  I didn\'t panic.  The next bill came with no credit, a late fee, interest and the collection calls started. On XXXX  XXXX, immediately after the Macy\'s bill arrived, I called and got no place with the agent except she removed the late fee.  I insisted that she have someone call me to discuss the fact that I did not get the credit that was promised.  On XXXX XXXX at XXXX I received a call from XXXX  from Macy\'s.  That recording is attached as XXXX.  She called back while I was at the doctor and asked my wife to have me return her call.  The next recording is not very useful, I am trying to get back to XXXX with no luck at all.  I then called a different number I had for Macy\'s claim and talked to XXXX.  He had information on my dispute from my prior email and opened a new dispute for me for XXXXXXXX  minus the XXXXXXXX  plus any late fees and interest.  That claim number is  XXXX XXXX XXXX. Accidently, I deleted my recording of his call. My Macy\'s card number isXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX.  I spent time talking to XXXX  from CFPB regarding this case.\nNOTE: I am having trouble uploading my voice recordings. I will try to add them later.'
